verdicts.title: Involuntary session-expiry (401) path leaves the previous user's trips/expenses/days/budgets in STATE and re-persists them to localStorage — shared-device privacy leak
verdicts.reasoning: core.ts:269-291's 401 branch only calls clearAuthToken()+STATE.user=null+emit, and clearAuthToken (core.ts:118-146) resets notifications/draft/geminiKey/settlements/achievements/prefs but NOT trips/archivedTrips/expenses/tripDays/budgets/activities/photos/savedFormats/categories/outbox, while saveState (subscribed at state.ts:438) re-serializes the WHOLE STATE 250ms later (state.ts:363), re-writing the prior user's data the blob-removal just cleared.

verdicts.title: isCreator omitted from /api/auth/google login response → Creator tab/card invisible to non-admin Creators until a full page reload
verdicts.reasoning: The /api/auth/google response user object (auth.py:262-275) has no isCreator field whereas /api/user-status (auth.py:82) does; STATE.user=data.user at auth.ts:58 (login) vs auth.ts:182 (restoreSession) are the ONLY two STATE.user reassignments and /api/data never touches it, so isCreatorUser() = !!STATE.user?.isCreator||isAdmin (Settings.tsx:79) is false for a non-admin creator until next boot, hiding the Creator tab (Settings.tsx:259) and card (Settings.tsx:371).

verdicts.title: auth_sessions rows are never reaped once their JWT expires (only revoked rows are swept) → Sessions UI lists phantom dead devices and the table grows unbounded
verdicts.reasoning: _create_session inserts a new row per login with no expiry (auth.py:199-203), the JWT has a finite 30-day exp (auth.py:253), list_sessions selects WHERE user_id=? AND revoked_at IS NULL with no created_at/expiry filter (auth.py:357-360), and the sweep deletes only revoked_at IS NOT NULL AND <now-30d (main.py:795-798), so expired-but-unrevoked rows are both listed and never reaped.

verdicts.title: Settings → Sessions panel is entirely hardcoded English (no t() calls) — es/fr/pt users see an untranslated device-management screen
verdicts.reasoning: Sessions.tsx hard-codes 'Active sessions' (123), 'Devices currently signed in...' (124-126), 'Loading…' (129), 'No active sessions found.' (131-132), 'This device' (168), 'Last active...Signed in' (179-180), 'Sign out'/'Revoke' (200) and the modal copy (92-98) as string literals with no t() lookups, and the comment at 118-120 explicitly self-documents the gap; only _formatRelativeTime routes through t().

verdicts.title: Sessions UI device-label re-parser (and its docstring) assume a raw User-Agent, but the server now stores a pre-summarized label — the regex parse is dead code that survives only by accidental fallback
verdicts.reasoning: The server stores a summarized 'Browser on OS' string via _summarize_ua() in _create_session (auth.py:192,135-173) which contains no 'Chrome/123' version token, but _deviceLabel (Sessions.tsx:50-62) only matches /Browser\/[\d.]+/ patterns so browser='' and it returns raw.slice(0,60) — working only by the fallback — and the docstring (Sessions.tsx:43-46) plus misc.ts still describe it as a raw UA.

verdicts.title: initGoogleLogin() renders the GIS button into a #googleBtnContainer element that does not exist anywhere — dead/misleading boot code
verdicts.reasoning: initGoogleLogin does getElementById('googleBtnContainer') (auth.ts:163) but a repo-wide grep of src+templates shows the only login button container is id='loginWallBtnContainer' (LoginWall.tsx:141) with no 'googleBtnContainer' anywhere, so that renderButton branch never runs and initGoogleLogin's only live effect is the ensureGsiInitialized() wiring.
